title: Busy in the Future
description: Talking about an action in progress at a specific future time.
lesson_content_markdown:
# What Will You Be Doing?

This form is for talking about what you will be **in the middle of** at a certain time in the future. We call this the 'Busy in the Future' tense. 

Instead of just saying "I will play soccer tomorrow," you say, "At 5:00 PM tomorrow, I **will be playing** soccer." This says you are busy with the action at that exact moment. 

### How to Make It
Use **WILL BE** + **-ING**:
- I **will be** research**ing**.
- The scientists **will be** conduct**ing** a test. 

### When to Use It
We use this when we talk about our **academic schedules** or long projects: 
- **At 10:00 AM next week**, all of us **will be taking** the English final test.
- **During the next three months**, the student group **will be working** on their project.

It feels more like a movie of the future. You are 'seeing' the person busy with the work at that time. It's great for showing that something takes a long time or will happen at a specific point on the map.

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Which future form shows the action will be happening at a specific time?

questions.question: At this time tomorrow, the student council _____ the new election rules.
Next Tuesday at 2:00 PM, the professor _____ a presentation in the main lecture hall.
The research team _____ the data while we are on summer break.
